message:
fix: key-routing fall-through for cross-view switching and HTTP response body leak guards

pkg/ui/model.go — key dispatch overhaul for cross-view navigation:

Previously, each focus-state case (focusInsights, focusBoard, focusGraph,
focusTree, focusActionable, focusHistory, focusSprint, focusFlowMatrix)
unconditionally dispatched ALL keys to its handler and returned immediately.
This meant view-toggle keys like 'b' (board), 'g' (graph), 'a' (actionable),
'i' (insights), 'E' (sprint), and 'f' (flow matrix) were swallowed — the
user had to first press Escape to return to focusList before switching views.

The fix replaces each blanket dispatch-and-return with a selective key switch:
each focus handler now explicitly enumerates the keys it actually uses (nav,
selection, editing, search, etc.) and sets a viewToggleHandled flag. Keys NOT
claimed by the handler naturally fall through to the view-toggle block below,
enabling direct cross-view switching without leaving the current focus first.

Key design decisions per view:
- focusBoard: sacrifices "g" (gg-combo for jump-to-top) so it falls through
  to the graph view toggle — board still has Home/G/ctrl+u for top navigation.
- focusTree: similarly sacrifices "g" (jump-to-top) in favor of graph toggle;
  tree retains Home/ctrl+u/pgup for equivalent navigation.
- focusHistory: adds modal awareness — when search is active or the file-tree
  has focus, ALL keys are routed to the handler (no fall-through), since those
  sub-modes need full keyboard control. Outside those modes, only history-
  specific keys (h/f/g/j/k/J/K/v/tab/enter/y/c/F/o/slash) are intercepted.
- focusInsights, focusGraph, focusActionable, focusSprint, focusFlowMatrix:
  each enumerates only its navigation/action keys and lets everything else
  fall through.
- focusLabelDashboard: already returned via its own path; 'h' and 'd' key
  checks now use the cached keyStr variable instead of repeated msg.String().
- focusDetail: 'O' (open-in-editor) check now uses cached keyStr.

A new `keyStr` variable caches msg.String() at the top of the block to avoid
repeated method calls, and `viewToggleHandled` gates the early return.

The comment block above the view-toggle switch is updated to reflect that
these keys are now reachable from ANY focus state (not just focusList).

pkg/updater/updater.go — HTTP robustness improvements:

1. Response body leak guards on error paths (3 call sites):
   In Go's net/http, when CheckRedirect returns a non-nil error, or when
   certain transport-level errors occur, the Response may be non-nil with an
   unclosed Body even though error is also non-nil. The existing code only
   had `defer resp.Body.Close()` after the error check, meaning the body
   would leak in the error branch. Added explicit nil-guarded resp.Body.Close()
   calls inside the `if err != nil` blocks for:
   - checkForUpdates(): the GitHub latest-release version check
   - GetLatestRelease(): the release metadata fetch
   - downloadFile(): the binary download (most critical, as redirect errors
     are common here)

2. Bounded download size in downloadFile():
   Previously, io.Copy read the entire response body with no upper bound,
   meaning a malicious or corrupted server response could write unbounded
   data to disk. Now wraps resp.Body in io.LimitReader when expectedSize > 0,
   capping at expectedSize + 10% (to allow for encoding variance) with a
   floor of 100MB to avoid overly tight limits on small binaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
